name: Nginx
summary: High-performance HTTP server, reverse proxy, and load balancer. The de facto standard for serving static files, TLS termination, and upstream proxying in production deployments.

name: Caddy
summary: Modern web server with automatic HTTPS via built-in ACME client. Zero-config TLS makes it ideal for small deployments and developer environments.

name: Traefik
summary: Cloud-native edge router with automatic service discovery for Docker and Kubernetes. Dynamic configuration eliminates manual route management in containerized environments.

name: HAProxy
summary: Reliable, high-performance TCP/HTTP load balancer used by major internet companies. Extremely low latency and resource usage under heavy traffic.

name: Envoy
summary: L7 proxy and communication bus designed for large service-oriented architectures. Foundation for Istio service mesh; excels at observability and traffic management.

name: Kong
summary: Cloud-native API gateway built on Nginx/OpenResty. Plugin ecosystem covers auth, rate limiting, logging, and transformations without custom code.

name: Apache APISIX
summary: Dynamic, real-time API gateway with rich plugin ecosystem. etcd-backed configuration enables hot reloads without restarts; strong alternative to Kong.

name: Django
summary: Batteries-included Python web framework with ORM, admin, auth, and migrations. Ideal for data-heavy applications where rapid development matters more than raw throughput.

name: Django REST Framework
summary: Powerful toolkit for building REST APIs on Django. Adds serializers, viewsets, authentication, and browsable API to Django's foundation.

name: Flask
summary: Lightweight WSGI micro-framework for Python. Minimal core with extension ecosystem; good for small APIs, prototypes, and ML model serving endpoints.

name: Starlette
summary: Lightweight ASGI framework/toolkit for Python async web services. Foundation underlying FastAPI; useful standalone for high-performance async applications.

name: Sanic
summary: Async Python web server and framework built for speed. Built-in server eliminates need for external ASGI server; supports HTTP/3.

name: Falcon
summary: Minimalist ASGI/WSGI framework optimized for building fast REST APIs and microservices. Extremely low overhead; ideal for performance-critical Python services.

name: Express.js
summary: Minimal and flexible Node.js web framework. De facto standard for Node backends; massive middleware ecosystem but showing age versus newer alternatives.

name: Fastify
summary: Fast and low-overhead Node.js web framework with built-in schema validation and serialization. Modern Express alternative with better performance and developer experience.

name: NestJS
summary: Progressive Node.js framework for building scalable server-side applications. Angular-inspired architecture with dependency injection, modules, and decorators.

name: Hono
summary: Ultrafast web framework for edge runtimes, Deno, Bun, and Node.js. Tiny footprint with Web Standard APIs; runs anywhere JavaScript runs.

name: Spring Boot
summary: Opinionated Java/Kotlin framework for production-grade applications. Auto-configuration and embedded servers make enterprise Java development accessible; massive ecosystem.

name: Quarkus
summary: Kubernetes-native Java framework with GraalVM native compilation support. Sub-second startup and low memory make it viable for serverless and container workloads.

name: Gin
summary: High-performance HTTP web framework for Go. Martini-like API with up to 40x better performance; the most popular Go web framework by GitHub stars.

name: Fiber
summary: Express-inspired Go web framework built on Fasthttp. Fastest Go framework in benchmarks; familiar API for developers coming from Node.js.

name: Actix-web
summary: Powerful, pragmatic Rust web framework. Consistently tops TechEmpower benchmarks; type-safe extractors and middleware with zero-cost abstractions.

name: Axum
summary: Ergonomic Rust web framework from the Tokio team. Tower-based middleware and extractors leverage Rust's type system for compile-time request validation.

name: Ruby on Rails
summary: Full-stack web framework emphasizing convention over configuration. Pioneered many patterns now standard across frameworks; still productive for CRUD-heavy applications.

name: Phoenix
summary: Productive Elixir web framework with real-time capabilities via channels and LiveView. BEAM VM provides fault tolerance and massive concurrency out of the box.

name: Keycloak
summary: Enterprise identity and access management with SSO, OIDC, SAML, and LDAP federation. Comprehensive auth solution that eliminates building custom identity systems.

name: Authentik
summary: Modern identity provider with flexible authentication flows. Visual flow designer and proxy provider make it simpler to deploy than Keycloak for smaller teams.

name: SuperTokens
summary: Open-source auth with pre-built UI components and session management. Drop-in replacement for Auth0 with self-hosted option; simpler than Keycloak.

name: Authelia
summary: SSO and 2FA portal for web application proxies. Lightweight alternative to Keycloak for homelab and small deployments using Nginx/Traefik auth forwarding.

name: Zitadel
summary: Cloud-native identity management with built-in multi-tenancy and CQRS/event-sourcing architecture. Modern alternative to Auth0 and Keycloak.

name: FusionAuth
summary: Developer-focused identity platform with registration, login, MFA, and SSO. Self-hosted Auth0 alternative with simpler deployment model than Keycloak.

name: Ory Hydra
summary: OAuth 2.0 and OpenID Connect certified server. Headless design with no built-in UI allows full customization of login/consent flows.

name: Ory Kratos
summary: Cloud-native identity and user management system. Handles registration, login, MFA, and account recovery with headless API; pairs with Ory Hydra for OAuth.

name: Vitess
summary: Database clustering system for horizontal scaling of MySQL. Powers YouTube's database infrastructure; adds sharding, connection pooling, and query routing to MySQL.

name: KeyDB
summary: Multithreaded fork of Redis with higher throughput on multi-core machines. Drop-in Redis replacement with active replication and FLASH storage support.

name: Typesense
summary: Fast, typo-tolerant search engine optimized for instant search-as-you-type. Simpler alternative to Elasticsearch for application search with built-in curation.

name: Zinc
summary: Lightweight full-text search engine with Elasticsearch-compatible API. Single binary deployment uses far fewer resources than Elasticsearch for small-medium datasets.

name: Jaeger
summary: End-to-end distributed tracing system originally from Uber. Visualizes request flows across microservices for latency analysis and root cause diagnosis.

name: Zipkin
summary: Distributed tracing system originally from Twitter. Simpler than Jaeger with lighter footprint; good for teams starting with distributed tracing.

name: Grafana Tempo
summary: High-scale distributed tracing backend requiring only object storage. No indexing overhead; pairs with Grafana for visualization and Loki for trace-to-log correlation.

name: Grafana Mimir
summary: Horizontally scalable long-term storage for Prometheus metrics. Successor to Cortex; handles billions of active series with multi-tenant isolation.

name: Thanos
summary: Highly available Prometheus setup with long-term storage via object storage. Sidecar model extends existing Prometheus deployments without replacing them.

name: SigNoz
summary: Unified observability platform covering metrics, traces, and logs with OpenTelemetry-native ingestion. Self-hosted Datadog alternative with ClickHouse backend.

name: Sentry
summary: Application monitoring and error tracking with stack traces, breadcrumbs, and release tracking. SDKs for every major language; essential for production debugging.

name: Datadog
summary: Comprehensive cloud monitoring and security platform with metrics, traces, logs, and APM. Industry-standard SaaS observability but significant cost at scale.

name: New Relic
summary: Full-stack observability platform with APM, infrastructure monitoring, and distributed tracing. Generous free tier; OpenTelemetry-native ingestion.

name: Uptime Kuma
summary: Self-hosted monitoring tool with a clean UI for HTTP, TCP, DNS, and ping checks. Lightweight alternative to StatusCake/Pingdom with notification integrations.

name: Netdata
summary: Real-time performance and health monitoring with per-second granularity. Zero-config auto-detection of services; extremely low overhead despite high-resolution metrics.

name: Nagios
summary: Legacy infrastructure monitoring with plugin-based architecture. Still widely deployed in enterprise; large plugin ecosystem but dated UI and config model.

name: Istio
summary: Full-featured service mesh for Kubernetes providing traffic management, security, and observability. Envoy-based data plane with rich but complex configuration model.

name: Linkerd
summary: Ultralight Kubernetes service mesh focused on simplicity and performance. Rust-based micro-proxy adds mTLS, observability, and reliability with minimal resource overhead.

name: Consul
summary: Service discovery, configuration, and segmentation solution. Multi-datacenter aware with built-in service mesh (Consul Connect); works across Kubernetes and VMs.

name: Vault
summary: Secrets management and data protection platform. Dynamic secrets, encryption as a service, and identity-based access control for infrastructure and applications.

name: Nomad
summary: Flexible workload orchestrator for containers, VMs, and standalone binaries. Simpler alternative to Kubernetes that works well for non-containerized and mixed workloads.

name: pgBouncer
summary: Lightweight connection pooler for PostgreSQL. Reduces connection overhead and improves throughput for applications with many short-lived database connections.

name: Patroni
summary: Template for PostgreSQL HA with automatic failover using etcd, Consul, or ZooKeeper for consensus. De facto standard for self-managed Postgres HA clusters.

name: ProxySQL
summary: High-performance MySQL proxy with query routing, caching, and connection multiplexing. Essential for MySQL HA setups with read/write splitting.

name: pgBackRest
summary: Reliable PostgreSQL backup and restore with parallel compression, encryption, and cloud storage support. Production-grade backup solution for any PostgreSQL deployment.

name: Velero
summary: Kubernetes cluster backup, restore, and migration tool. Backs up cluster resources and persistent volumes; essential for disaster recovery in Kubernetes environments.

name: Restic
summary: Fast, secure, and efficient backup program with deduplication and encryption. Supports multiple cloud backends; simple CLI makes it scriptable for HPC environments.

name: BorgBackup
summary: Deduplicating archiver with compression and encryption. Mature and battle-tested for server backups; efficient for datasets with many similar files.

name: Tyk
summary: API gateway and management platform with developer portal, analytics, and rate limiting. Go-based alternative to Kong with lower resource footprint.

name: Pomerium
summary: Identity-aware access proxy implementing zero-trust principles. BeyondCorp-style access without VPN; authenticates and authorizes every request to internal services.

name: Apache SkyWalking
summary: APM system for distributed systems with service mesh observability. Auto-instrumentation agents for Java, Python, Node.js, and Go; strong in cloud-native environments.

name: Gravitee
summary: API management platform with gateway, developer portal, and access management. Supports REST, GraphQL, gRPC, and event-driven APIs in a unified platform.

name: Casdoor
summary: UI-first identity access management platform with built-in UI for login, registration, and admin. Supports OAuth 2.0, OIDC, SAML, and 40+ social login providers.

name: Logto
summary: Modern Auth0 alternative with beautiful pre-built sign-in UI, multi-tenancy, and RBAC. Developer-friendly with SDKs for major frameworks.

name: Koa
summary: Expressive middleware framework for Node.js by the Express team. Uses async/await natively; smaller and more modular than Express with cleaner error handling.

name: Micronaut
summary: JVM framework with compile-time dependency injection and AOP. Near-instant startup and low memory consumption; designed for microservices and serverless.

name: Vert.x
summary: Reactive toolkit for building event-driven applications on the JVM. Polyglot (Java, Kotlin, Groovy, JS); excels at high-concurrency with non-blocking I/O.

name: Aiohttp
summary: Async HTTP client/server for Python built on asyncio. Both client and server in one package; mature choice for Python async web services and HTTP clients.

name: Tornado
summary: Python web framework and asynchronous networking library. Originally from FriendFeed; handles long-polling, WebSockets, and long-lived connections efficiently.

name: Connexion
summary: OpenAPI-first framework for Python that generates routes and validation from your spec. Ensures API implementation matches documentation by design.

name: Rocket
summary: Type-safe Rust web framework with focus on usability. Derive macros for routing and form handling make Rust web development more accessible.

name: Echo
summary: High-performance minimalist Go web framework with automatic TLS and HTTP/2. Clean API for building RESTful services with middleware support.

name: Ktor
summary: Asynchronous Kotlin framework for building server and client applications. Coroutine-based with pluggable engine; idiomatic choice for Kotlin backend development.

name: Kiali
summary: Observability console for Istio service mesh with topology visualization, traffic flow analysis, and configuration validation. Essential companion for Istio deployments.

name: Coroot
summary: eBPF-powered observability with automatic service map discovery and anomaly detection. Zero-instrumentation monitoring that maps dependencies from network traffic.

name: Sidekiq
summary: Efficient background job processing for Ruby. Redis-backed with multi-threaded execution; the standard for async work in Rails applications.

name: Dramatiq
summary: Simple and reliable distributed task processing for Python. Modern Celery alternative with better defaults, priority queues, and rate limiting built in.

name: Huey
summary: Lightweight Python task queue with minimal dependencies. Redis-backed; ideal when Celery is overkill and you need simple periodic or deferred task execution.

name: Minio Operator
summary: Kubernetes-native operator for deploying and managing MinIO tenants. Automates provisioning, TLS, and multi-tenant object storage on Kubernetes.

name: cert-manager
summary: Kubernetes-native certificate management controller. Automates issuance and renewal of TLS certificates from Let's Encrypt, Vault, and other CAs.

name: Kyverno
summary: Kubernetes-native policy management without new languages. Policies as Kubernetes resources using familiar YAML; validates, mutates, and generates configurations.

name: Cilium
summary: eBPF-based networking, security, and observability for Kubernetes. Kernel-level packet processing delivers high performance with network policies and transparent encryption.

name: KEDA
summary: Kubernetes Event-Driven Autoscaling based on external metrics. Scales workloads from zero to N based on queue depth, database queries, or custom metrics.

name: Argo Rollouts
summary: Progressive delivery controller for Kubernetes with canary and blue-green deployments. Integrates with service meshes and ingress controllers for traffic splitting.

name: External Secrets Operator
summary: Kubernetes operator that syncs secrets from external stores (Vault, AWS SSM, GCP Secret Manager) into Kubernetes Secrets. Single interface to multiple secret backends.
